principle: Use cards for lists where every item shares the same structure and behaviors — design the max-content and min-content variants first to ensure the layout works at both extremes before filling in the rest
explanation: X.ui|cards: use_when: items_share_same_behaviors(image+title+description+action)+need_responsive_reflow; design_process: mock_up_max-content_item+min-content_item -> tweak_until_both_readable -> fill_rest; decide: icon_actions(save_space) vs text_actions(clarity); benefit: responsive_reflow+scannable+action-ready; NO:cards_when_items_have_completely_different_structures NO:different_primary_actions_per_card_in_same_list; eg: Pinterest+Airbnb+news_articles -> perfect_card_candidates >> mixed_structure_items -> use_table_or_list

principle: Use a carousel for horizontally browsable visual items when vertical space is scarce — it encourages serendipitous discovery but prevents jumping deep into the list, so avoid it for task-critical navigation
explanation: X.ui|carousel: use_when: visual_items_uniquely_identified_by_image+similar_size+flat_list+limited_vertical_space; benefit: compact_vertical_footprint+engaging_horizontal_browsing; limitation: user_cannot_jump_to_deep_position -> must_scroll_through_everything -> encourages_serendipity; add_arrows_to_signal_more_content_exists; NO:carousel_for_task-critical_navigation NO:carousel_hiding_all_but_one_item_with_arrows(defeats_purpose); eg: Apple_TV_row+Netflix_genre_rows -> compact+browsable >> full_grid -> vertical_space_consumed

principle: Encode critical data distinctions using preattentive visual variables — color, size, shape, and orientation are processed in parallel before conscious attention, so key data points pop out regardless of how many items surround them
explanation: X.ui|preattentive_variables: preattentive_variables: color+size+shape+orientation+position+motion+enclosure+line_weight; key_property: processed_parallel(constant_find_time) NOT linear_scan(read_every_value); use_multiple_variables_for_multidimensional_data; application: make_outliers_differ_on_preattentive_variable -> user_finds_instantly; NO:all_data_points_same_visual_weight(forces_scanning_every_value) NO:expecting_users_to_count_or_compare_raw_numbers; eg: 100_numbers_find_values>1 -> read_each_one >> large_font_for_outliers -> found_immediately

principle: Match the data visualization model to the data structure — the same dataset shown as a table versus a map reveals completely different patterns, and the wrong model actively hides insight
explanation: X.ui|dataviz_model: models: linear(list)+tabular(spreadsheet)+hierarchical(tree)+network(flowchart)+geographic(map/scatter)+textual(word_cloud); selection_rule: if_data_fits_multiple_models -> consider_which_plays_up_which_aspects; wrong_model: geographic_data_as_table -> geographic_patterns_invisible; goal: let_users_draw_own_conclusions(show_not_tell); NO:always_defaulting_to_table_for_any_data NO:forcing_spatial_data_into_text_list; eg: sales_by_region_as_spreadsheet >> sales_by_region_as_map -> cluster_patterns_visible_instantly

principle: Build UI systems bottom-up from atoms to molecules to organisms — a style change at the atomic level propagates automatically to every component that inherits it, making global updates instantaneous
explanation: X.ui|atomic_design: hierarchy: atoms(text_field+color+typeface) -> molecules(input+label+hint+button) -> organisms(full_header_with_nav+search+avatar) -> templates -> pages; key_property: styling_inherits_upward -> change_color_token_once -> updates_everywhere; style_guide_first: color+typography+grid+icons; benefit: consistency+scalability+reusability_across_all_devices; NO:building_screens_without_component_system NO:one-off_styles_per_organism; eg: change_primary_button_color_in_one_token >> propagates_to_all_organisms_automatically
